data.reasoningText:
Let me understand what needs to be migrated:

1. **xunit v2.9.3** → **xunit.v3** (the main xUnit package)
2. **xunit.runner.visualstudio v2.8.2** → **xunit.runner.visualstudio v3.x** (or the new runner)
3. **Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk** → may still be needed or may change
4. **Xunit.Combinatorial v1.6.24** → need to check v3 compatibility
5. **Xunit.StaFact v1.1.11** → need to check v3 compatibility


6. The runner package name changes to `xunit.v3.runner.visualstudio`, and the test SDK dependency gets removed since v3 handles its own entry point. The project also needs to be configured as an executable rather than a class library, though the test attributes like `[Fact]` and `[Theory]` stay the same.

I should verify whether `Xunit.Combinatorial` and `Xunit.StaFact` have v3-compatible versions available on NuGet, since those are dependencies I'll need to account for in the migration. `[StaTheory]` attributes are available in Xunit.StaFact version 2.0.x for v3 compatibility. Let me check NuGet to confirm the current versions of these packages.

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

Now I have a good picture. Let me determine the right versions:

1. **xunit.v3**: Latest stable is 3.2.2 (since it depends on xunit.v3.mtp-v1 = 3.2.2)
2. **Xunit.Combinatorial**: v2.0.24 is for xunit.v3 v1.x (depends on xunit.v3.extensibility.core >= 1.0.0). But xunit.v3 is now at v3.2.2... Let me check.

Wait, the Xunit.StaFact page says:


- v2.0 → xUnit.v3 v1.x
- v2.1+ → xUnit.v3 v2+
- v3.0+ → xunit.v3 v3+

So for xunit.v3 3.2.2, I need Xunit.StaFact v3.0.13 (which depends on xunit.v3.extensibility.core >= 3.0.0). For Xunit.Combinatorial, v2.0.24 only supports xunit.v3 v1.x, so I'll need to find a newer version that's compatible with v3.x. I need to look up the specific xunit.v3 versions to confirm compatibility.

Confirmed that xUnit v3 test projects need to be executables with `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`, and the entry point is automatically generated so I don't need to add a `Main` method. Now I'll apply these changes and verify everything builds correctly.
